summary: #54716 remains the canonical repair path for #54435, but it is not merge-ready. The branch is maintainer-editable and narrow, so Clownfish should repair the contributor branch rather than replace it. No GitHub mutations are planned because the job blocks comment, label, close, merge, force-push, and bypass actions.

actions.reason: Repair the existing contributor branch before any merge or closeout decision.
Keep the issue open until #54716 or an equivalent fix lands and validation passes.
Produce the narrow repair plan for the deterministic executor.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #54716 so literal per-agent session store paths resolve as one scoped shared agents-root store, while true literal single-file stores keep current behavior.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix: repair literal per-agent session store discovery
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- continue #54716 by keeping literal `agents/<id>/sessions/sessions.json` configs on the multi-agent discovery path
- scope discovery to the literal path's agents root so default state sessions do not leak in
- preserve the resolved literal store path and derive the agent id from the on-disk store path before legacy key canonicalization

## Credit
This repair carries forward the fix and proof from @giulio-leone in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/54716.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- Codex `/review` clean after the repair
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @giulio-leone and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/54716.
PR body should state that the repair continues the narrow fix from #54716 and carries attribution forward.
Release-note context is user-facing because sessions.list/dashboard session visibility changes for literal per-agent store configs.
